title: Tapping for Hosting Anxiety
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for before people arrive
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the hosting anxiety that fires up before guests arrive. The script works on the pressure to have the house right, the measuring against an imaginary standard, and the gap between preparing well and preparing enough.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the hour before guests arrive when the house does not feel ready and the anxiety is mounting. Also good the day before a gathering.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the anxiety that arrives before guests do. The script addresses the pressure to have the house right, the imaginary standard you are measuring against, and the permission to welcome people in a room that is good enough.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though the house is not how I want it and people are coming, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I want everything to be right before I can relax, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I want the house to look perfect before I can relax, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** The house is not how I want it.
**Side of eye:** They will see the bits I have not got to.
**Under eye:** I have been preparing and it is still not enough.
**Under nose:** The hosting anxiety is loud.
**Chin:** I care too much about what they will think.
**Collarbone:** I want it to be right before I can let myself enjoy any of this.
**Under arm:** The house is never quite ready when people are coming.
**Top of head:** The anxiety is bigger than the house.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** They are coming to see me, not the house.
**Side of eye:** I have done what I can do.
**Under eye:** A clean enough floor is enough.
**Under nose:** Nobody is measuring my home the way I am measuring it.
**Chin:** I can let the rest go.
**Collarbone:** My welcome matters more than my walls.
**Under arm:** I can be a good host in an imperfect room.
**Top of head:** The house is ready enough. So am I.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
